title: Leucosceptrum ningpoense
taxonomy: Leucosceptrum ningpoense is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. This plant is native to China and is also known as Ningpo fir-leaved spirea. It is an evergreen shrub that grows up to 1-2 meters tall. The plant's leaves are arranged in a opposite and decussate pattern, with a lanceolate shape and entire margin. Leucosceptrum ningpoense is often grown as an ornamental plant due to its attractive foliage and white flowers.

intro: Leucosceptrum ningpoense, also known as Ningpo fothergilla, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to China and characterized by its clusters of small, white or purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Leucosceptrum ningpoense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese herbal medicine, Leucosceptrum ningpoense is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in combination with other herbs to make patent medicines, such as cough syrups and expectorants.
